          Bio
          In broad academic terms, my education and experience make
          me a geographer, but practically speaking I do geospatial data
          science. I’m currently a Data Scientist with the Arête Glacier
          Initiative where I’m working to build better forecasts of
          sea-level rise.
          Previously, I spent 6+ years researching applications of
          remote sensing in forest ecology and management. I carried out
          this research as a Postdoctoral Scholar with Oregon State
          University and the USFS RMRS Forest
          Inventory and Analysis unit in Riverdale, Utah, and as
          Research Assistant with the Climate and Applied Forest
          Research Institute while I earned my Ph.D. at SUNY ESF.
          The bulk of that work is reflected in the papers and presentations tabs on this
          site.
          
          
          Education
          SUNY College of Environmental Science &
          Forestry | Syracuse, NY
          Ph.D. in Environmental Science | August 2019 - May 2024
          Tufts University | Medford, MA
          B.S. in Computer Science | August 2013 - May 2017
          
          
          Experience
          Data Scientist | Arête Glacier Initiative
          | April 2026 - Present
          Postdoctoral Scholar | Oregon State
          University | August 2024 - April 2026
          Research Assistant | SUNY ESF, CAFRI | August 2019 - August
          2024
          Data Engineer | Lightkeeper, LLC | August
          2017 - July 2019
